memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but starting the term project now feels like too much pressure. I'll just grab some leisure time instead and handle it later.
Lunch with the cohort feels like a relief from this project pressure. Hope they don't make it awkward.
Relaxing felt good, but the project is still stuck. My cohort won't notice, and I can fix it later.
I waited for the project to catch up, hoping my friends wouldn't notice. It's easier to ignore the pressure now.
Waiting felt safer than pushing through, but the silence is heavy. Hope my friends won't notice I'm just stalling.
Waiting feels safer, but the heavy silence makes me worry they'll notice. I hope to fix it later without stressing anyone.
I just waited again, hoping they won't notice the delay. The silence is heavy, but it feels safer than risking their reaction.
I kept waiting because the silence feels safer than risking their reaction, even though I'm running on empty.
Waiting again feels safer, but the heavy silence is getting louder. I'm running on empty and hope they don't notice my hesitation.
Waiting again feels safer, but the silence is getting louder. I'm running on empty and hope they don't notice my hesitation.
The silence is deafening now, and I'm terrified to speak because any reaction might ruin our connection. I'm running on fumes but can't bring myself to risk it.
The silence is crushing me now; I'm too drained to risk anything, even though waiting feels safer than the pain of connection.

desires: Keep the silence and avoid speaking until I feel physically stronger, because any risk of rejection from my close friends feels worse than doing nothing.
Complete that trivial chore tonight just to get a small win without having to explain it or ask for permission, so I don't look unreliable tomorrow.

goals.description: Maintain the 'heavy silence' with cohort_3 and teammate_A until I feel physically stronger, ensuring no one notices I'm mentally checking out.
goals.rationale: Breaking the silence now would look like panic or rejection. Keeping it thick protects my image as someone who is just deep in thought. It's better to let them think I'm fine than to admit I'm running on empty before we hit the real pressure point.

goals.description: Do the trivial chore tonight just to get a small win without having to explain it or ask for permission.
goals.rationale: Doing this right now ruins the vibe; I need that social buffer first. But once they're gone, I can't afford to drag it out into tomorrow because then I'll be stuck explaining why I was 'busy' all day. A quick win later means less guilt, but not today.

agent.last_reasoning: Keeping the heavy silence with cohort_3 is priority. Breaking it now risks looking panicked or rejecting them, which hurts closeness more than a little study progress would.

action.name: message

args.content: Hey! Free for lunch today? Just wanted to check in and grab a bite.

action.reasoning: This advances the high-priority goal of showing engagement with cohort_3, which I value far more than the term project. It avoids friction by keeping it casual, and since they're close, it maintains our bond without needing deep commitment right now.
